data.title: Nomura moves to capture Japan’s booming institutional crypto market
data.description:
Securities company Nomura Holdings is preparing to expand into Japan’s cryptocurrency market through its Switzerland-based subsidiary, Laser Digital Holdings.

A Laser Digital spokesperson confirmed Friday that the unit is in pre-consultation talks with Japan’s Financial Services Agency (FSA) and intends to apply for a license to offer crypto trading services to institutional investors in the country. 

However, the spokesperson told Cointelegraph that the application date remains “undetermined,” and will depend on the outcome of the discussions with the FSA. 

If approved, Laser Digital would launch broker-dealer services for traditional financial firms and crypto-focused companies, including exchanges in Japan.

Japanese institutions plan to invest in crypto

The move follows a broader push by institutions into the Japanese crypto market.

Earlier this week, Daiwa Securities Group, one of Japan’s largest brokerages, introduced a crypto lending service that allows clients to borrow Japanese yen using Bitcoin and Ether as collateral. 

Laser Digital and Daiwa’s push into the Japanese market seems to be a response to growing demand for crypto investments in the region. 

In June, Nomura and Laser Digital unveiled the results of a survey exploring institutional appetite for crypto. The survey showed 54% of investment managers — including family offices, corporations and institutional investors — expect to invest in cryptocurrencies within the next three years.

Japan regulator proposes crypto rule overhaul

Alongside rising institutional interest, Japan’s regulatory stance on crypto has also shifted in a more favorable direction.

Japan has looked to reform its crypto laws to align the sector’s rules with those of its traditional securities market, and also plans to lower taxes on crypto. In August, the country’s regulators also quietly greenlit the country’s first stablecoin pegged to the Japanese yen. 

As the country’s policies shifted, crypto adoption in the country more than doubled.

On Sept. 24, a Chainalysis report showed that among the five markets in the Asia Pacific (APAC) region, Japan saw the strongest growth. The country’s value received onchain grew 120% year-on-year in the 12 months leading to June. 

Chainalysis’s head of APAC policy, Chengyi Ong, previously told Cointelegraph that Japanese crypto market activity reflected global trends like the “sharp pickup in trading volume” after the US presidential election.

data.title: Alphabet Rolls Out Gemini 2.5 Flash AI and Viral Nano Banana Tool
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL, Financials) is leaning into the playful side of artificial intelligence with the wider release of its Gemini 2.5 Flash model and the viral Nano Banana tool that's helping it win over millions of users.

The company said that Gemini 2.5 Flash is now available for production use, offering developers support for 10 aspect ratios that make it easier to create content across social platforms. At the same time, Nano Banana, a quirky image-generation tool that lets users create animated GIFs and edits, has gone viral and helped Gemini surpass OpenAI's ChatGPT as the most downloaded free app in Apple's App Store.

Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai leaned into the buzz, encouraging developers to go bananas in a post on X.

For investors, the momentum is meaningful. The surge in Gemini adoption has added energy to Alphabet's AI story, helping lift the company past a $3 trillion market value last month. Analysts say the release underscores how Google is balancing infrastructure investments with consumer-facing AI products in its battle with OpenAI and Meta.

data.title: Plug Power gains after H.C. Wainwright raises PT
data.description:
** Shares of hydrogen fuel cell maker Plug Power rises 11.3% at $3.15 premarket

** H.C. Wainwright hikes PT to $7 from $3, citing rising electricity prices and stronger regulatory support for nuclear power

** New PT represents nearly 150% upside to PLUG's last close, ties it with Northland Securities for the highest among Wall Street

** Brokerage notes robust data center power demand and industrial electrification pushes power costs up, making green hydrogen more price-competitive and supporting its adoption

** Says U.S government's support for nuclear energy, especially small modular reactors could help accelerate adoption of green hydrogen as this provides stable and carbon-free power enabling cheaper hydrogen production

** Seven of 25 brokerages rate stock "buy", 13 "hold" and five "sell" or lower; their median PT is $1.8 - data compiled by LSEG

** Up to last close, PLUG up nearly 33% YTD

data.title: Kenya raises $1.5 bln as part of debt buy-back plan
data.description:
By Duncan Miriri

Kenya raised $1.5 billion via new 7-year 7.875% and 12-year 8.8% bonds, the finance ministry said on Friday, as part of an effort to extend its debt maturities and cut refinancing costs.

The combined 8.7% interest rate was 1% lower than it would have paid at the start of the year, Chris Kiptoo, principal secretary at the Treasury said, while there had been strong investor demand with offers of more than $7.5 billion.

This "move eases pressure on taxpayers, boosts investor confidence and creates fiscal space to fund key development priorities," he said in a statement.

The government is buying back $1 billion of a February 2028 bond and it will use some of the proceeds to finance the repurchase.

The East African country has been working to cut its overall debt, which stands at close to 70% of its GDP, and make repayments more manageable.

The government has revamped its debt maturity management after its shilling currency weakened sharply in the run-up to a big Eurobond maturity in June last year due to market concerns about its ability to pay off that bond.

The new plan involves issuing amortising bonds to avoid the kind of bullet payment it faced last year.

Earlier this year, it also bought back another bond maturing in 2027 as part of the strategy of managing near-term refinancing risks proactively.

The government is also turning to innovative tools to manage its debt load, including a plan to issue a pioneering $1 billion debt-for-food-security swap by March next year.

A team of staff from the International Monetary Fund is currently in Nairobi for talks on a new Fund-supported programme after the expiry of the last one in April.

data.title: Concentrix Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Concentrix Corp, a leading global provider of business services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the three months ended August 31, 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and operational efficiency, showcasing significant growth in key areas despite facing various market challenges.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: Concentrix Corp reported revenue of $2,483 million, marking a 4.0% increase compared to the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by the company's expansion across its primary industry verticals.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $855 million, which represents a slight decrease of 1.1% from the previous year. The gross margin declined to 34.4% from 36.2%, attributed to changes in revenue and cost structure.

Operating Income: Operating income stood at $147 million, a decrease of 4.1% compared to the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily due to the reduction in gross profit.

Net Income: Net income saw a significant increase, reaching $88 million, up from $17 million in the same period of the previous year. This reflects the company's improved financial performance.

Basic EPS: Basic earnings per share (EPS) rose to $1.34, a substantial increase from $0.25 in the same period of 2024, indicating strong earnings growth.

Diluted EPS: Diluted EPS also increased to $1.34, consistent with the basic EPS rise.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Concentrix Corp experienced revenue growth across its primary industry verticals. The technology and consumer electronics vertical saw a 0.9% increase, retail, travel, and e-commerce grew by 4.9%, communications and media increased by 8.1%, and banking, financial services, and insurance rose by 9.1%. The healthcare vertical experienced a 1.0% increase, while the 'other' category saw a 1.5% decrease.

Geographical Performance: Approximately 89% of the company's consolidated revenue for the nine months ended August 31, 2025, was generated from non-U.S. operations, with 54% of this revenue priced in U.S. dollars. The company benefited from favorable foreign currency exchange rates, particularly due to the strengthening of the euro against the U.S. dollar.

Operational Efficiency: The company managed to decrease its selling, general, and administrative expenses by 0.4% for the three months ended August 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024. This was achieved despite an increase in share-based compensation expenses and underlying business expenses.

Future Outlook: Concentrix Corp anticipates continued revenue generation from its non-U.S. operations, with a significant portion priced in U.S. dollars. The company expects to face ongoing impacts from foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations and inflation, particularly in regions where it operates delivery centers.

Cost Management: The company experienced a 6.9% increase in the cost of revenue for the three months ended August 31, 2025, primarily due to increased volumes and foreign currency exchange rate changes. However, it managed to reduce acquisition-related and integration expenses significantly.

Market Trends: Concentrix Corp operates in a competitive industry, facing pricing and labor cost pressures. The company is impacted by economic conditions in key markets such as the Philippines, India, Brazil, and the United States, and is subject to geopolitical and economic risks in these regions.

Seasonality: The company noted that its revenue and margins typically peak in the fourth fiscal quarter, reflecting underlying trends in its clients' businesses and consumer activity levels.

SEC Filing: Concentrix Corp [ CNXC ] - 10-Q - Oct. 03, 2025

data.title: Goldman Sachs Initiated at Market Perform by BMO Capital
Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0808 ET - Stainless steel company Aperam would benefit from the tougher trade measures set to be unveiled by the EU next week, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. About 20% of all stainless steel consumed in the EU is imported, the analysts say. With import barriers raising, companies like Aperam or peer ArcelorMittal could sell more steel domestically and achieve higher spreads, or better margins per metric ton of steel sold, the analysts say. In carbon-steel, ArcelorMittal offers the most compelling upside on a risk-adjusted basis, Morgan Stanley says. Aperam is the bank's preferred stainless steel play in context of EU's tougher measures. Shares are up 3.7% at 31.60 euros. (maitane.sardon@wsj.com)

0803 ET - The outlook for Amazon's cloud-computing unit, Amazon Web Services, is beginning to improve, D.A. Davidson says in a research note. Analysts Gil Luria and Alexander Platt say proprietary data show a pickup in September activity, buoyed by "a notable uptick in growth both in core hyperscaler services and in AI-services," though they caution this was preceded by subdued activity in July and August. As a result, the analysts aren't expecting blowout F3Q results later this month. "We will be tracking activity throughout the beginning parts of October to see if these positive trends continue," they write. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0752 ET - Canadian software company Open Text's noncore divestiture is a positive move as the company refocuses on its main businesses, according to Scotiabank. Open Text agreed to divest a part of its analytics portfolio to NetDocuments for $163 million in cash. In a report, analyst Kevin Krishnaratne says the strategy highlights the software company's move to "grow by shrinking." Krishnaratne says it aligns with a plan of rationalizing its portfolio to concentrate on its core secure information management for AI platform. The analyst thinks there may be one divestiture per quarter, which will strengthen Open Text's balance sheet while allowing management to focus on higher-growth areas. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

0732 ET - Investor concerns are growing over French telecommunications operators' potential inability to capitalize on a possible SFR breakup, JP Morgan Global Equity Research analyst Akhil Dattani writes in a note. Altice France owner Patrick Drahi is considering breaking up and selling SFR to rivals Orange, Bouygues Telecom, and Iliad to reduce Altice's debt, according to media reports. Orange and Bouygues shares have surged in the year to date, partly on hopes of domestic consolidation, Dattani says. However, doubts are emerging about whether these operators can ultimately seize the opportunity, unsettling investors. Still, the conditions remain favorable for a deal, he says. The transaction might serve the mutual interests of all parties, he adds. Orange and Bouygues shares are down 1.1% and 1.3%, respectively. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0731 ET - China-focused equity funds this week recorded their largest inflows since April, driven by tech-related enthusiasm, TD Securities strategists say in a note. U.S. equity funds also posted strong inflows, the strategists say. "U.S. equity funds nearly matched last week's inflows ($6.7 billion versus $6.5 billion)." (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0707 ET - Oil prices could fall as OPEC+ is expected to agree to a production increase during the weekend, but the implications for the currency market look unclear, MUFG Bank's Derek Halpenny says in a note. With the U.S. a significant crude oil producer, a decline in prices can coincide with a weaker dollar--especially if the price decline is supply increase related, he says. In the case of a demand shock that dents risk sentiment, the dollar could benefit from safe haven flows. For the euro, lower oil prices would be positive from a real incomes perspective. If the drop in crude oil was large enough, however, it could give the European Central Bank scope to cut interest rates again, he says. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0646 ET - India's economy will likely hold up despite U.S. tariffs, Shivaan Tandon of Capital Economics says in a note. The Reserve Bank of India "appears increasingly willing to rely on channels beyond just interest-rate cuts to support activity," the economist writes. While the RBI left rates unchanged this week, it proposed various ways to support the banking sector to enhance Indian lenders' resilience and competitiveness while boosting credit flow, Tandon notes. The RBI also presented a package of regulatory proposals, several of which are aimed at boosting bank lending. These efforts should help the economy post healthy growth rates in the years ahead, Tandon adds. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0641 ET - Galderma could upgrade its sales outlook for the current year, driven by the success of its dermatitis drug Nemluvio, UBS analysts say in a research note. In July, the Swiss skincare company raised its full-year guidance for net sales to range between 12% and 14% on-year growth at constant currency. "We would expect a further guidance at 3Q given the continued strong trajectory of Nemluvio," the analysts say. UBS forecasts Nemluvio sales of $110 million in the U.S. and $122 million worldwide during the quarter. Galderma said it would reinvest the proceeds from Nemluvio in advertising, so the margin forecast should remain the same, they say. Shares are up 1.5% at 139.90 Swiss francs. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0625 ET - Markets are shrugging off concerns about the U.S. government shutdown, resulting in low volatility in global equities and bonds markets, Moneyfarm's Richard Flax says in a note. "Our baseline view is to look past what's likely to be political noise," he says. An extended shutdown, however, could lead to fewer official statistics being released, making investment decisions harder, Flax says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0619 ET - Vestas Wind Systems announced a flurry of onshore orders late in the third quarter, but a lack of offshore orders will weigh on the average selling price and could mean margins disappoint, Deutsche Bank analyst John Kim writes. The bank expects the Danish wind-turbine maker to report total third-quarter orders worth 4.9 billion euros, equivalent to 4.8 gigawatts, with revenue of 5.5 billion euros and deliveries of 4.4 gigawatts. It estimates group adjusted EBIT at 301 million euros, with a margin of 5.5%, versus consensus at 6.2%. It updates its forecasts for currencies and trading conditions, lifts its target price to 115 Danish kroner from 118 kroner and keeps its hold rating. Shares fall 3.3% to 123.15 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0614 ET - EssilorLuxottica looks set to maintain its positive performance, helped by the success of smart glasses, analysts at UBS write in a note. The analysts expect the Ray-Ban maker to report an acceleration in sales for the third quarter, driven by good results in the key North American market, the analysts say. The eyewear group should continue to benefit from new launches and the popularity of smart glasses until its competitors launch a product with comparable features, they say. The stock trades 0.6% higher at 282 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0600 ET - Kering's recent management changes and creative shifts seem positive, but a more positive outlook in the luxury sector would give it a boost, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. Earlier this year, the French luxury-goods giant named a new CEO and appointed a new creative chief of Gucci, its flagship label, as it continues to reinvigorate its brands. While these moves seem to boost turnaround hopes, the group could do with some help from an improving industry narrative, Jefferies says. Just like Kering, other luxury players are grappling with a downturn in demand for high-end goods. Jefferies forecasts a decline in third-quarter sales of 9.1% on year on a comparable basis, with Gucci down 15.5%. Shares trade down 0.2% at 286.30 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)
